project_summary: A system for citizens to register complaints with the police, which police can then investigate and manage. The system includes features for help, defect tracking, robust security with unalterable audit trails, role-based access control, and detailed reporting. The solution must adhere to strict availability, performance, scalability, and usability/accessibility standards, including ISO 9241 and WCAG 1.0.

functional.text: Citizens can register their complaints with police, and police shall take the complaint forward based on evidence, facts, and investigation.
The solution should provide an interface for the user to log any defects or enhancement requests on the application and track them thereafter.
The solution should send alerts, such as email or SMS, to the user if the user chooses to whenever any action has been taken on the alert.
The solution should enable the help-desk user to view reports on submitted defects or enhancement requests category-wise, status-wise, and age-wise.
The System must be able to export audit trails for specified cases without affecting the audit trail stored by the System.
The System must at a minimum be able to provide reports for actions on cases organised by case.
The System should be able to provide reports for actions on cases organised by workstation and, where technically appropriate, by network address.
The System must allow the user to limit access to cases to specified users or user groups.
The system should provide for role-based control for functionality and must allow a user to be a member of more than one group.

If a user requests or searches for a case they do not have the right to access, the System must provide a configurable response: either display title and metadata, or not include such cases in any count of search results.
If a user performs a quick or advanced search, the System must never include in the search result list any record which the user does not have the right to access.
The System must be able to display several entities (cases, suspects) simultaneously.
The System must allow persistent, user-definable defaults for data entry.
If user-specified profiles are used, users should be able to see, modify and delete that profile on demand.

non_functional.text: The solution should provide detailed context-sensitive help material for all possible actions and scenarios on all user interfaces.

The System must keep an unalterable audit trail capable of automatically capturing and storing information about all actions (create/read/update/delete) on critical entities (case, suspect, property) and the user initiating the action.
Once activated, the System must track events automatically without manual intervention and store them in the audit trail.
If the System allows users to make unauthorised attempts to access cases, it must log these in the audit trail.
Any access to cases, and all other activities involving cases and related documents or data, should be stored in the audit trail.
All error messages produced by the System must be meaningful, so that they can be appropriately acted upon by users.
The System must employ a single set of user interface rules, or a small number of sets, to provide a familiar and common look and feel.
The interfaces must be made customizable or user-configurable to the extent possible, and such configurations must be saved in the user profile.
The System must provide End User and Administrator functions which are easy to use and intuitive throughout.
Frequently-executed System transactions must be designed so that they can be completed with a small number of interactions.
Where the System employs a graphical user interface, it must allow users to customise it, including menu contents.
The user interfaces should be designed to make them user-intuitive.
The user interfaces of the system should comply with Standard ISO 9241.
The system must be suitable for users with special needs and comply with accessibility standards: ISO 9241-20 (ICT), ISO 9241-171 (Software), and WCAG 1.0 (Content).
The System must be available to users from <xx:00> to <xx:00>.
The planned downtime for the System must not exceed <xx> hours per rolling three month period.
Unplanned downtime for the System must not exceed <xx hours/minutes> per rolling three month period.
The number of incidents of unplanned downtime for the System must not exceed <x> per rolling three month period.
In the event of any software or hardware failure, it must be possible to restore the System (with inline synchronization) within no more than <xx> hours.
The System must be able to retrieve and display within 5-8 seconds a case which has been accessed within the previous 2 months.
The System must be able to retrieve and display within 20 seconds a case which has not been accessed within the previous 2 months.
The System must be scaleable and must not have any features which would preclude use in small or large police stations, with varying numbers of cases handled.
The system should be designed in a manner that operational data is not lost in case of any failure of equipment or communication network.

data.text: The word 'unalterable' means the audit trail data cannot be modified in any way or deleted by any user.
Audit trail data may be subject to re-department and copying to removable media if required, so long as its contents remain unchanged.
The System must maintain the audit trail for at least the life of the case to which it refers.
The System must be able to capture and store violations.

business_rules.text: The System must allow only admin-users to set up user profiles and allocate users to groups.
The System must allow changes to security attributes for groups or users (such as access rights, security level, privileges, password allocation and management) to be made only by a super-user.

interfaces.text: The support solution should be accessible to users both from within the application and also outside the application through a browser interface.
The system should support a multilingual interface.

constraints.text: The user interfaces of the system should follow the guidelines specified under www.usability.gov.
When designing multimedia information presentations, the design principles and recommendations described in ISO 14915-1 to ISO 14915-3 should be taken into account.
For screen text legibility, ISO 9241-303 shall be consulted for requirements.

User interface design must adhere to specific usability guidelines, including but not limited to: providing text equivalents for non-text media, self-descriptive navigation, minimizing navigation effort, avoiding unnecessary windows and horizontal scrolling, designing for input device independence, using color carefully, consistent layout, providing a sitemap, and linking back to a home page.
